relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The Father's love names us children of Divine.
What we will be is not yet manifest, but seeing him makes likeness.
Hope purifies.
Misalignment is lawlessness.
Messiah was manifested to take misalignment away.
Remaining in him changes the pattern of life.
Doing justice shows justice.
The Son appears to dissolve adversarial works.
Divine seed remains in the one born of Divine.
Children of Divine are known by justice and sibling-love.
The beginning message is love one another.
Cain warns against hatred of the just sibling.
Do not marvel at the world-system's hatred.
Love of siblings marks passage from death to life.
Hatred is death-dealing.
Love lays down life for siblings.
Closed compassion toward need reveals love's absence.
Love in work and truth, not merely word.
Truth persuades the heart before Divine.
The Divine is greater than the condemning heart.
A heart at peace has boldness toward Divine.
Asking flows from keeping and pleasing.
The command is trust in Jesus Messiah and love one another.
Keeping the command is mutual remaining known by Spirit.

contemplative_note: 1 John 3 is love made visible: Divine children do justice, open compassion, and love in work and truth while resting in a Divine greater than the accusing heart.
